---
# v2.8.0 design-brief.md schema PROTOTYPE
# Generated by ds-couture field test 2026-04-29

project: ciso-daily-controls
version: 1
date: 2026-04-29
designer: ds-couture v2.7.2

# 1. Design Context Protocol (5 required inputs)
audience:
  persona: CISO Daily Controls
  basis: new persona derived via ds-ref-personas fallback (Sarah role × Yuki job)
  device: 27" desk monitor
  lighting: indoor office daylight
  time_budget_minutes: 3-5
  time_of_day: morning brief
job_to_be_done: Validate that controls (EDR, SIEM, patching) are operating
tone:
  concrete: [authoritative, quiet, selvsikker]
  meta: premium
  dominant: authoritative
anti_references:
  - AI-generic SOC wall
  - rainbow-pie cybersec dashboards (Splunk demo aesthetic)
brand:
  primary_hex: "#C8102E"
  secondary_hex: <missing — derived as cool-blue if needed>
  neutral_hex: <missing — using canonical light tokens>
  wordmark: ACME
  font_display: "GT Sectra, Charter, Georgia, serif"
  font_body: "(Splunk default — system sans, fallback for Söhne)"

# 2. Archetype + flavor commit
archetype: executive summary, controls-status flavor
canvas:
  width: 1440
  height: 960
theme: light
aesthetic_flavor: refined
density: low

# 3. Palette (refined-light)
palette:
  canvas: "#FAFAF7"
  panel_card: "#FFFFFF"
  panel_stroke: "#E5E5E0"
  text_primary: "#1A1A1A"
  text_secondary: "#6B7C85"
  gridline: "#EBEDEF"
  axis: "#D9DCE0"
  brand_primary: "#C8102E"
  status_critical: "#C0392B"
  status_warning: "#D4820A"
  status_ok: "#2B9E44"
  status_info: "#2066C0"

# 4. Brand-color collision resolution
brand_collision:
  trigger: brand-red vs status-red collision
  resolution: step-4-wordmark-only-plus-4px-stripe
  forbidden:
    - no brand-red as canvas
    - no brand-red as panel fill
    - no brand-red zone-band ≥40px
  enforcement_locations:
    - header zone only (wordmark + 4px stripe)

# 5. Layout zones
layout:
  outer_margin: 32
  inner_gutter: 24
  zones:
    - name: brand_header
      y: 32
      h: 44
      panels: [brand_wordmark, brand_stripe]
    - name: section_posture
      y: 104
      h: 32
      panels: [section_posture_md]
    - name: kpi_strip
      y: 152
      h: 180
      panels: [kpi_critical_hero, kpi_edr, kpi_patch, kpi_triaged]
      hierarchy: hero (kpi_critical) + 3 supporting
      hero_ratio: 1.5x
    - name: section_trend_controls
      y: 364
      h: 32
      panels: [section_trend_md]
    - name: primary_content
      y: 412
      h: 380
      golden_ratio: true
      panels: [trend_line (hero, w=836), controls_table (supporting, w=516)]
    - name: footer
      y: 824
      h: 104
      panels: [footer_escalation_md]

# 6. Drilldowns (Scope Check #1)
drilldowns_planned:
  kpi_critical: "/incidents?severity=critical&status=open"
  kpi_edr: "/edr-coverage?status=non-compliant"
  kpi_patch: "/patch-status?status=missing"
  kpi_triaged: "/triage-history?range=today"
  trend_line: "/incidents-day?date=$click.value$"
  controls_table: "/control-detail?control_id=$row.control_id$"

# 7. Markdown zones (Scope Check #2)
markdown_zones:
  - brand_wordmark    # identity
  - section_posture   # narrative for KPI cluster
  - section_trend     # narrative for trend+controls cluster
  - footer_escalation # operational handoff

# 8. Depth pattern (Scope Check #3)
depth_pattern: every-panel-card
description: "All 11 panels render as #FFFFFF cards on #FAFAF7 canvas with R_CARD=8 + 1px #E5E5E0 stroke. Inconsistent depth = automatic reject."

# 9. Tabs (Scope Check #4)
tabs_considered: yes
tabs_decision: rejected
tabs_waiver: |
  Tabs considered and rejected because exec daily-brief job benefits from
  predictable single-view repetition; tab-navigation breaks the 3-5 min
  scan budget. Daily morning brief = same view every day; predictability
  is the value.

# 10. Footer present (Scope Check #6)
footer_present: yes
footer_content:
  - runbook link
  - on-call (PagerDuty schedule)
  - escalation Slack channel
  - owner email
note: archetype is exec (controls-status); footer is overspec relative to canonical exec but legitimately useful for CISO escalation.

# 11. Slop Test status
slop_test:
  status: PASS
  pass_count: 13
  fail_count: 0
  fixes_applied:
    - id: "Slop Test #5"
      issue: KPI row had no visual hierarchy (4 equal-sized KPIs)
      resolution: promoted KPI 1 to hero (w=434, FS_KPI_HERO 72px); KPIs 2-4 supporting (w=290, FS_KPI_MAJOR 48px)
  pending_at_handoff:
    - id: "Slop Test #8"
      commit: "All searches will be time-bounded with earliest/latest in SPL or via dashboard-level defaults block."

# 12. Scope Check status
scope_check:
  status: PASS-WITH-WAIVERS
  pass_count: 6
  fail_count: 0
  fixes_applied:
    - id: "Scope Check #2"
      issue: KPI cluster and primary-content cluster lacked descriptive section markdowns
      resolution: added 2 markdown panels with FS_XLARGE section headers
  waivers:
    - id: "Scope Check #4"
      gate: tabs explicitly considered
      decision: rejected
      reason: "exec daily-brief benefits from predictable single-view repetition; tab-navigation breaks 3-5 min scan budget"

# 13. Hand-off readiness
handoff_ready: true
handoff_blockers: []
handoff_notes:
  - All entity-displaying panels have drilldowns wired
  - All status-tokens paired with redundant encoding (label + icon in controls grid)
  - Brand/status color zones isolated
  - Panel cards consistent across all 11 panels
  - 11 panels < 12-panel exec ceiling
---

# Why these decisions

## Why CISO Daily Controls (new persona)

The 8 pre-defined personas in `ds-ref-personas` did not match cleanly. CISO Sarah is right-role / wrong-context (iPad / 90s / board narrative). Compliance Officer Yuki is right-job-to-be-done / wrong-role / wrong-time-budget. The fallback procedure produced a hybrid: CISO at desk monitor, daily 3-5 min budget, control-validation focus.

## Why refined flavor (not editorial, not luxury)

Tone words `authoritative + quiet + selvsikker` with brand-red constrained to wordmark. Editorial leans narrative-typography-driven; we have minimal narrative content. Luxury wants heavy serifs + champagne-gold (wrong for daily ops). Refined (Stripe-style) is single accent + tinted neutrals + professional spacing — exactly the brief.

## Why hero KPI on Active Critical Incidents

The hero is the headline number for "are controls working?" — a non-zero value triggers the daily decision to escalate. The other three KPIs (EDR, Patch, Alerts) are secondary signals that inform "what's the focus today" but do not by themselves trigger a paging-grade decision.

## Why no tabs

Considered: tabs for "Today / This Week" or "Posture / Activity / Compliance". Rejected because:
1. Daily morning brief = same view every day → predictability is the value
2. CISO has 3-5 min budget → tab navigation costs ~5 sec per switch
3. Job is single-purpose (validate today) — not multi-mode exploration

## Why every-panel-card depth (not background-rectangle)

Refined-flavor light-mode canonical = panel-as-card-on-warm-canvas (Stripe / Notion pattern). Background-rectangle pattern (one card behind a panel cluster) belongs to industrial / SOC archetypes where density wins. For exec-low-density, panel-as-card creates breathing room and deliberate-craft signal.

## Why brand-wordmark-only treatment

Brand-red `#C8102E` collides with status-red `#C0392B` (both saturated reds at H≈25-27). Brand-collision tree priority order requires demoting brand presence below status presence. Step-1 (brand-secondary) and step-2 (brand-neutral) unavailable (only primary supplied). Step-3 (4px stripe under header) and step-4 (wordmark-only) both safe; step-4 chosen because refined-light wants minimal brand-trykk.

## Open issues for v2.8.0

- This brief is a hand-written prototype. v2.8.0 production schema should:
  1. Be enforced by ds-create (refuse to start without brief)
  2. Have explicit `<missing>` markers that fail validation
  3. Include version field for schema evolution
  4. Track waiver count + reasons in machine-readable form

- Brand secondary + neutral are `<missing>`. Real production design would prompt user explicitly during Design Context Protocol when primary is collision-color.

- This brief was written AFTER the design decisions, not BEFORE. v2.8.0 production flow should write the brief incrementally as decisions are made, with each decision-point gated on prior brief sections.
